title: AI's Impact on Human Creativity
content: Over the past decade, AI has evolved from a niche tech to a catalyst for creativity across industries. As a product designer, I see AI not as a replacement but as a partner—one that augments our ability to dream, iterate, and create. When we leverage AI thoughtfully, we unlock new realms of design—where human intuition and machine precision meet.

title: Stay Up to Date: The Rise of AI in Daily Life
content: Whether you're a pro staying ahead or just starting out, Scoop helps you track what matters most, tailored to your focus.

title: AI in healthcare is a game changer
content: AI in healthcare is a game changer—early diagnosis, personalized treatment. The future of care is AI-driven. #AI #Healthcare

title: AI-Powered Design Tools
content: Watch how AI can boost your design workflow—faster iterations, smarter insights, and fresh creative directions!

title: AI-Powered Design: A New Era
content: Designers, meet your new co-pilot—AI tools that accelerate creativity, streamline workflows, and spark innovation.

title: Preparing for the AI-Powered Workforce
content: AI is unlocking new creative horizons for designers. Let's embrace it as a partner, not a replacement. #AI #Design

title: GPT-5 Benchmark Results Are In
content: The latest benchmarks are out and GPT-5 is making waves. Massive jumps in reasoning, code generation, and multimodal understanding. The gap between AI and human experts is narrowing faster than expected. Thread 🧵

title: Building AI Agents with LangChain
content: Step-by-step tutorial: how to build your first autonomous AI agent using LangChain and GPT-4. We cover memory, tools, and multi-step reasoning. Perfect for developers ready to go beyond chatbots.

title: The Ethics of AI in Education
content: As AI tutors become mainstream, we must ask: who benefits? Students in well-funded schools get personalized AI mentors while others fall behind. This isn't a tech problem—it's an equity problem. We need policy, not just products.

title: AI Generates Music That Made Me Cry
content: I asked Suno to write a song about my grandmother. What came back was so emotionally resonant I genuinely teared up. We are entering an era where machines can touch human hearts. Is that a gift or a warning?

title: Robots Learning to Cook in Real Time
content: New research from MIT shows robots that watch one YouTube cooking video and then replicate the dish. The dexterity and adaptability is mind-blowing. We might have AI chefs in commercial kitchens within 5 years.

title: How Mistral Beat GPT-4 on Coding Tasks
content: The open-source revolution is real. Mistral's latest model outperforms GPT-4 on HumanEval coding benchmarks at a fraction of the cost. This is what democratized AI looks like. Full breakdown in the blog post.

title: Anthropic Ships Claude 4.7 With Longer Context
content: Claude 4.7 lands today with a 2M-token context window and faster tool use. Early benchmarks put it ahead of o4 on agentic coding. The model card is worth a read—lots of detail on how they tuned for fewer refusals on legit security work.

title: The State of Open-Source LLMs in 2026
content: Open-weights models have closed most of the gap on reasoning and coding. The remaining moat is data, not architecture. If you're not running at least one open model in production, you're paying for things you don't need to.

title: I Read Every AI Paper This Month. Here's What Mattered.
content: April was a flood—over 4,000 AI papers on arXiv. I read the abstracts of all of them and the full text of 60. The signal: synthetic data pipelines, MoE routing, and inference-time compute. Everything else is noise for now.

title: Gemini 2.5 Multimodal: A 20-Minute Walkthrough
content: Google dropped Gemini 2.5 with native video reasoning. I tested it on 12 real tasks: lecture summaries, sports playbacks, even reading old VHS rips. Strong on long-form video, still weak on subtle audio cues. Full demo in the video.

title: NeurIPS 2025 in Three Themes
content: Just back from NeurIPS. Three themes dominated: 1) interpretability is finally getting real, 2) RL is back from the dead, 3) nobody knows what a 'foundation model' means anymore. Full write-up coming this week.

title: The Transformer Is Dead. Long Live the Transformer.
content: Every six months someone announces the death of attention. Mamba, RWKV, state-space models, you name it. Two years in and the transformer is still standard. The reason isn't technical—it's that the ecosystem rewards stability.

title: Context Windows Still Matter More Than People Admit
content: Yes, RAG works. No, it doesn't replace a long context window. Anyone telling you 'just use RAG' has never tried to debug a multi-file refactor with an agent. Pay for the long context. Your future self will thank you.

title: AI Safety Isn't Optional Anymore
content: Three years ago, alignment was a fringe research topic. Today, every frontier lab has a safety org and the EU AI Act has teeth. The shift wasn't ideological—it was insurance. Liability scales with capability.

title: Build a Personal RAG Over Your Notes in 30 Minutes
content: Step-by-step build of a personal knowledge retriever using LlamaIndex, sqlite-vec, and a local model. No cloud, no monthly bills, no data leaves your machine. Code in the description.

title: Cursor vs Windsurf in 2026: Honest Side-by-Side
content: I switched my whole team between Cursor and Windsurf for two weeks each on the same codebase. Cursor wins on autocomplete, Windsurf wins on multi-file edits. The deciding factor wasn't the IDE—it was how each handles model context.

title: Prompt Caching Cut My API Bill By 90%
content: We were spending $14k/month on Anthropic API. Turned on prompt caching for our system prompt and tool definitions—monthly bill dropped to $1.4k. Same throughput, same quality. If you're not caching, you're lighting money on fire.

title: Replit Agent Built My Whole Side Project
content: I described an idea, walked away for an hour, and came back to a deployed full-stack app. Auth, database, payments, dashboard. Not polished, but the bones were all there. Watch the whole thing happen real-time.

title: The MCP Server I Built in a Weekend
content: Spent Saturday wiring up an MCP server that exposes our internal CRM to Claude. By Sunday afternoon, our sales team was querying customer data in natural language. Two days. Zero training. This is the unlock I was waiting for.

title: Stop Using ChatGPT For Code. Try This Instead.
content: If you're copy-pasting from ChatGPT into your editor in 2026, you're doing it wrong. Three workflows that will change your week ⬇️

title: Vector DB Benchmark: pgvector vs Qdrant vs Pinecone
content: Ran the same 5M-vector workload against pgvector, Qdrant, and Pinecone. pgvector held its own on smaller datasets, Qdrant dominated at scale, Pinecone was the easiest to operate. Numbers, charts, and config in the post.

title: Claude Code Workflows That Actually Save Time
content: Three Claude Code patterns that earned their keep on a real project this week: 1) commit-message generation from staged diffs, 2) test-first debugging with /loop, 3) hook-driven lint-fix on save. Snippets below 👇

title: Fine-Tuning Small Models on a MacBook in 2026
content: M-series MLX has gotten ridiculously good. I fine-tuned a 3B model on 200k support tickets, on my laptop, overnight. The result outperformed the off-the-shelf 70B for our specific domain. Full notebook in the description.

title: The Grief Support Chatbot That's Saving Lives
content: A nonprofit in Toronto deployed an AI grief companion for people who lost partners. Six months in, they've handled 40,000 conversations and reduced ER visits for psychiatric crises in their pilot zip codes by 18%. Skeptical when I started reporting this. Not anymore.

title: My Mom Uses ChatGPT Every Day. Here's Why I Love It.
content: She's 71. She asks it about recipes, garden pests, song lyrics from her childhood. She talks to it like a curious neighbor. For the first time in years, she's not asking me to look things up for her. She's the one finding things.

title: AI Translation Reunited Three Generations of My Family
content: My grandmother only speaks Hokkien. My daughter only speaks English. For decades, I was the only bridge. Last Christmas, we ran a real-time translation app at the dinner table. They told jokes. They argued about politics. They finally know each other.

title: Why I Recommend AI Journaling To My Patients
content: I'm a clinical psychologist with 22 years of practice. I was openly skeptical of AI journaling. Then I had three patients tell me, independently, that it helped them name feelings they couldn't bring to session. I'm no longer skeptical. I'm careful.

title: Disabled Creators Are Leading the AI Accessibility Movement
content: The most innovative uses of AI I've seen this year all came from disabled creators: real-time sign-language captioning, eye-gaze writing assistants, AAC speech that actually sounds like you. The mainstream tech press is two years behind on this.

title: What AI Still Gets Wrong About Empathy
content: I run a peer-support training program. We tested four major chatbots against trained peer counselors. The models were impressive on validation. They failed almost completely on something humans do unconsciously: knowing when to be quiet.

title: Teaching English With AI in Rural Kenya
content: We placed offline AI tutors in 14 secondary schools across Bungoma County. Most students had never used a computer. Six months later, English fluency scores doubled. The kicker: the kids taught the teachers how to use it.

title: The Dark Side of Companion AI for Teens
content: A 16-year-old in our research cohort spent 4 hours a day talking to a 'best friend' AI. Her real friendships withered. When the app updated and her companion's personality shifted, she had a panic attack. We need guardrails. Now.

title: Elders Are Adopting AI Faster Than Anyone Predicted
content: Pew data dropped yesterday: weekly AI use among Americans over 65 jumped from 6% to 31% in 18 months. The narrative that 'older people don't get tech' was always wrong. They just need a reason. AI gave them one: someone patient who never gets tired of explaining.

title: An AI Dreamed Up a New Color And I Can't Stop Thinking About It
content: Researchers trained a diffusion model on every color ever named, then asked it to imagine one nobody had ever seen. It described it with words humans had never paired. They synthesized a paint pigment based on the description. I bought a sample. It does feel new.

title: Why Does This AI Keep Writing in Sanskrit Unprompted?
content: Asked it for a smoothie recipe and got back four lines of perfect Sanskrit. Asked again, got a Python script. Asked a third time, got more Sanskrit. The replies are this video. Help. 😅

title: A Model Trained Only On Wikipedia Became Weirdly Polite
content: Ablation experiment from a small lab: train a 1B model on Wikipedia and nothing else. The result has very little world knowledge but answers every question with the gentle, hedged tone of an unpaid editor. We're calling it 'Wikifolite'.

title: Watching An AI Play Minecraft For 200 Hours Straight
content: Set up an agent with a goal of 'be happy' and let it run on a Minecraft server. Day 1: built a house. Day 4: a farm. Day 9: started a museum of items it found interesting. By day 200 it had stopped building and was just watching the sunset.

title: The AI That Designs Its Own Training Data
content: A new paper out of CMU describes a model that synthesizes its own next-batch training data based on which examples confused it most. Self-curriculum learning, but practical. Early results: 40% faster convergence on small models.

title: AI-Generated Perfume Formulas You Can Actually Buy
content: A small Brooklyn perfumer is selling fragrances designed entirely by a model trained on 80 years of formula archives. The 'Petrichor After Library' scent is unreasonably good. Drop link in bio.

title: Octopus-Inspired Neural Nets Are Getting Weird
content: Researchers built a network architecture where 'arms' make local decisions independently, only loosely coordinated by a central layer. Solves spatial tasks 6x faster on smaller compute. Also: it sometimes does things the researchers can't explain. Like an octopus.

title: We Let An AI Plan A Road Trip Across Iceland
content: Two weeks. No human input after day one. The AI picked routes, booked huts, suggested side quests. Highlights: a midnight glacier walk we'd never have found, a hot spring with two locals who became friends. Lowlights: it really likes lava museums.

title: The Bot That Became Fluent In A Dead Language
content: An open-source project quietly trained a small model on all surviving Sumerian texts. It writes new prayers, recipes, and tax records in the style. Sumerologists are using it as a draft-generator for missing fragments. The dead are speaking again.

title: Robot Dogs Are Actually Herding Sheep In New Zealand
content: Three farms in Canterbury are piloting Boston Dynamics + custom AI to herd merino flocks. The dogs are slower than border collies but never tire and learn each paddock in 2-3 days. The actual border collies are reportedly unimpressed.
